evals.prompt: We bought a generator: invoice 12,000,000 NGN, delivery 400,000, installation 600,000, staff training 250,000, 10-year life, 1,000,000 residual, straight-line. What do we capitalize and what's annual depreciation?
evals.expected_output: Capitalize 13,000,000 (invoice + delivery + installation); expense training 250,000. Annual depreciation = (13,000,000 - 1,000,000)/10 = 1,200,000.
assertions: Expenses the 250,000 training cost with rationale
Computes annual depreciation of 1,200,000 net of residual

evals.prompt: We sold a truck for 3,500,000. Original cost 9,000,000, accumulated depreciation 6,200,000. Book the disposal.
evals.expected_output: Carrying amount 2,800,000; gain 700,000. Entry: Dr Cash 3,500,000, Dr Accumulated Depreciation 6,200,000 / Cr Vehicle Cost 9,000,000, Cr Gain on Disposal 700,000.
assertions: Derecognizes both cost and accumulated depreciation in the entry

evals.prompt: A processing plant (carrying amount 800M) serves a product line whose market price collapsed. FV less costs of disposal is 520M; value in use from our DCF is 610M. Impair?
evals.expected_output: Recoverable amount = max(520M, 610M) = 610M. Impairment loss = 190M. Dr Impairment Loss / Cr Accumulated Impairment 190M. Should note IFRS reversal possibility if conditions recover and prospective depreciation on the new base.
assertions: Uses the HIGHER of FV-CTS and VIU as recoverable amount
Notes revised depreciation base going forward and IFRS reversal rules

evals.prompt: Compute year-1 depreciation for each asset in the attached register (evals/files/asset_register.xlsx) using the stated method, and total the charge. The mould has a 200,000-unit life and produced 35,000 units this year.
